architecture.summary: The system applies cluster normalization to activation pairs before unsupervised probing. Model activations are clustered and normalized to reduce the impact of salient but unrelated features in the dataset, then passed to an unsupervised probe (e.g., Contrast-Consistent Search). The normalized activations form the input to the probe's knowledge extraction procedure.

biblio.title: Cluster-Norm for Unsupervised Probing of Knowledge

claims.statement: Cluster normalization significantly improves the ability of unsupervised probes to identify the intended knowledge amidst distractions.
This approach does not address the issue of differentiating between knowledge in general and simulated knowledge—a major issue in the literature of latent knowledge elicitation.
Salient but unrelated features in a given dataset can mislead unsupervised probes.
